title: HABITAT FOR HUMANITY NEWBERG AREA - 211info
text: NEWBERG AREA HABITAT FOR HUMANITY HABITAT FOR HUMANITY NEWBERG AREA Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Habitat for Humanity houses are built with volunteer labor and use many donated materials. Families contribute 500 hours (minimum) of "sweat equity" working alongside volunteers during construction of their homes. After a house is complete, it is sold to the family at an affordable base price. Mortgage payments received from homeowners are recycled into a Fund for Humanity to build more homes in the area. 620 N Morton Street Newberg, OR 97132 Eligibility: Call for information Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-1pm Email (503) 537-9938 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers Reception (503) 537-9938 Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Call for information FEES: Varies by service: mortgage payments are capped at 30% of homebuyers' gross monthly income with closing costs and with a 0% interest rate DOCUMENTS: None required at intake LANGUAGES: English, Spanish AREA SERVED: Cities of Dayton, Dundee, Newberg, St. Paul, and Sherwood Services: Home Purchase/Construction Financing/Refinancing Subsidized Home Acquisition Other Services Offered At This Location: INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - A BRUSH WITH KINDNESS INDIVIDUAL AND FAMILY SUPPORT SERVICES - HEALTHY HOMES If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service.
